title: Prenatal exposure to endocrine disrupting chemicals in relation to thyroid hormone levels in infants - a Dutch prospective cohort study.
ehr_evidence: Information on T4 levels in heel prick blood spots was obtained through the neonatal screening programme in the Netherlands.
summary: A Dutch prospective cohort study assessing the association between prenatal exposure to various endocrine disrupting chemicals (EDCs) and thyroxine (T4) levels in newborns.
key_findings: Girls in the highest quartile of DDE and PFOA exposure showed an increased T4 level.
Boys in the second quartile of exposure for both PFOS and PFOA showed a lower T4 level, though this was attenuated after full adjustment.
No effects were observed for other compounds.
population: newborns in a mother-child cohort in the Netherlands (n=83)
